---
title: "Quickstart Guide: nof1power"
author: "Ronald (Ryy) G. Thomas"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Quickstart Guide: nof1power}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  eval = FALSE
)
```

## Overview

**nof1power** is a reproducible research compendium for N-of-1 trial power
analysis and simulation. The project is built on the zzcollab framework for
Docker-based reproducibility.

## Prerequisites

- [Docker Desktop](https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop)
- Git

## Installation

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/rgt47/nof1power.git
cd nof1power

# Build the Docker image (one-time setup)
make docker-build
```

## Quick Start

### Run the Analysis

```bash
# Start R session in Docker
make r

# Or run analysis script directly
make r < analysis/scripts/analysis.R

# Or render a report
make r < 'rmarkdown::render("analysis/report/report.Rmd")'
```

### Interactive Development

```bash
# Enter development container with R session
make docker-zsh

# Or start RStudio Server
make docker-rstudio
# Access at http://localhost:8787
```

## Project Structure

```
nof1power/
├── analysis/
│   ├── data/               # Raw and derived data
│   ├── scripts/            # Analysis code
│   ├── report/             # Manuscript
│   └── figures/            # Generated visualizations
├── R/                      # Reusable R functions
├── tests/                  # Unit tests
├── Dockerfile              # Computational environment
└── renv.lock               # Package versions
```

## Daily Development Workflow

```bash
# 1. Enter development container
make docker-zsh

# 2. Work in R (packages auto-captured on exit)
library(tidyverse)
# ... your analysis ...

# 3. Exit container (automatic snapshot)
q()

# 4. Test and commit
make docker-test
git add .
git commit -m "Add analysis"
git push
```

## Common Tasks

```bash
# Start interactive R session
make docker-zsh

# Start RStudio Server
make docker-rstudio

# Run all tests
make docker-test

# Build Docker image
make docker-build

# Validate package dependencies
make check-renv

# Show all available targets
make help
```

## Adding Packages

Inside a running container:

```r
# Install a package
install.packages("package_name")

# Exit (automatic snapshot on exit)
q()
```

The `.Rprofile` automatically calls `renv::snapshot()` on exit to capture
new packages.

## Reproducibility

The project ensures reproducibility through five version-controlled
components:

1. **Dockerfile** - Computational environment
2. **renv.lock** - Exact R package versions
3. **.Rprofile** - R session configuration
4. **Source code** - Analysis scripts and functions
5. **Data** - Raw and derived datasets

## Team Collaboration

```bash
# Pull team Docker image and setup
zzcollab -u

# Start development environment
make docker-zsh
```

## Getting Help

```bash
# Brief overview
zzcollab help

# Specific topics
zzcollab help quickstart     # Solo developer guide
zzcollab help workflow       # Daily development
zzcollab help docker         # Docker details
zzcollab help renv           # Package management

# List all topics
zzcollab help --all
```

## Next Steps

- `analysis/data/README.md` - Data documentation
- `docs/` - Technical documentation
- `CLAUDE.md` - Development guidance
